info.title: Shipmozo Shipping API
info.description:
REST API for order fulfillment, courier assignment, tracking, warehouses, NDR, and labels. All responses use `result` (`1` = success, `0` = failure), `message`, and `data`.

**Base URL:** `https://shipping-api.com/app/api/v1` — do not add a trailing slash.

**Auth:** Send `public-key` and `private-key` headers on every protected route, or obtain keys via `POST /login`.

**Rate limits:** Shipmozo returns `x-ratelimit-limit` (500) and `x-ratelimit-remaining` on each response. Quota is **500 requests per 1-minute window** per API key; `remaining` drops with each call and **refreshes when the minute rolls over**.

**Support:** munish@apporio.in

x-rateLimit.windowBehavior: x-ratelimit-remaining only changes when Shipmozo sends a new HTTP response to you — it does not tick down on its own in the browser. Each API call lowers remaining by 1. After ~60 seconds, the next call you make usually shows remaining closer to 500 again (sliding window, not a live clock on the docs page).

x-ratelimit-remaining only changes when Shipmozo sends a new HTTP response to you — it does not tick down on its own in the browser. Each API call lowers remaining by 1. After ~60 seconds, the next call you make usually shows remaining closer to 500 again (sliding window, not a live clock on the docs page).

ApiKeyPublic.name: public-key
ApiKeyPublic.description: API public key from panel profile or POST /login

ApiKeyPrivate.name: private-key
ApiKeyPrivate.description: API private key — keep server-side only

rateLimitHeaders.title: Response headers (from live API / Swagger)
rateLimitHeaders.description: Shipmozo documents rate limits via response headers, not only in PDF copy. Read these on every call:

headers.name: x-ratelimit-limit
headers.meaning: Max requests allowed in the current 1-minute window for your API key

headers.name: x-ratelimit-remaining
headers.meaning: Requests left in the current window — decreases by 1 on each API call you make. You only see a new number on the next HTTP response (the page does not auto-update). After ~60s without traffic, the next call usually shows remaining closer to 500 again (sliding window).

rateLimitHeaders.note: 498 in tutorials means '2 calls used this minute' — not a fixed cap. Use the portal 'Check live rate limit' or Execute API to read current headers.

errorCodes.when: Seller KYC not completed or pending approval on Shipmozo panel
errorCodes.action: Finish verification in Shipmozo panel — keys are valid; APIs stay blocked until account is approved
Re-login via POST /login or verify panel keys

errorCodes.when: order_id unknown for assign, schedule, cancel, get-detail
errorCodes.action: Push order first; use same order_id as on your store
Use unique order_id or fetch existing via get-order-detail
Choose different courier or pincode pair

errorCodes.when: auto-assign-order without panel Settings > Auto assign
errorCodes.action: Enable auto-assign in Shipmozo panel or use assign-courier
Use awb_number from assign-courier or auto-assign response
Fix request body per parameter table

errorCodes.when: x-ratelimit-remaining hits 0 within the 1-minute window or HTTP 429
errorCodes.action: Pause calls; retry after ~60s when remaining rises again

workflows.title: Forward shipment (auto-assign)
steps: Configure Settings → Auto assign in Shipmozo panel
POST /auto-assign-order — AWB + courier in one call

glossary.meaning: Your store's unique order ID; must match across push, assign, and cancel
Air Waybill tracking number from courier
Pickup location ID from get-warehouses / create-warehouse
YES = skip schedule-pickup; NO = call schedule-pickup after assign

rateLimitGlobal.windowBehavior: x-ratelimit-remaining only changes when Shipmozo sends a new HTTP response to you — it does not tick down on its own in the browser. Each API call lowers remaining by 1. After ~60 seconds, the next call you make usually shows remaining closer to 500 again (sliding window, not a live clock on the docs page).
rateLimitGlobal.whenExceeded: HTTP 429 and/or result=0 with rate-limit message — wait until remaining rises again (typically within ~1 minute)
rateLimitGlobal.source: Observed on shipping-api.com production (Cloudflare); limit=500, remaining decrements per call and refills each minute

rateLimitsByEndpoint.notes: No API keys; still returns rate-limit headers
Query param awb_number; prefer webhooks over tight polling
Cache keys after success; do not poll login
Rate shopping; shares 500 pool with all APIs
